title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Here’s what happened in crypto today
briefing: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: From Bitcoin critics to blockchain believers: The 5 biggest crypto backflips
summary: From Bitcoin critics to blockchain believers: The 5 biggest crypto backflips
briefing: If you can't beat 'em, join 'em. Here are five crypto skeptics who have reluctantly embraced the opportunity of making loads of money with blockchain.

title: Strategy unveils capital framework to preserve Bitcoin exposure, pay dividends
summary: Strategy unveils capital framework to preserve Bitcoin exposure, pay dividends
briefing: Michael Saylor’s Strategy unveiled a capital framework allowing Bitcoin sales to fund dividends, a $2.55 billion reserve and buybacks while raising STRC payout to 12%.

title: Breez launches Bitcoin-to-stablecoin payments across more than 30 blockchains
summary: Breez launches Bitcoin-to-stablecoin payments across more than 30 blockchains
briefing: The new SDK feature lets developers route payments from Bitcoin balances to recipients in USDC and USDT without requiring users to hold stablecoins.

title: Prediction-market operational consolidation could spur M&A wave: Bernstein
summary: Prediction-market operational consolidation could spur M&A wave: Bernstein
briefing: Bernstein says platforms are bringing exchange, clearing and brokerage infrastructure in-house, setting up potential deals while increasing antitrust and regulatory risks.

title: Vitalik Buterin says obfuscation could unlock private onchain crypto voting
summary: Vitalik Buterin says obfuscation could unlock private onchain crypto voting
briefing: Vitalik Buterin said indistinguishability obfuscation could eventually support private, collusion-resistant onchain voting without trusted committees, though the technology remains impractical.

title: Germany leads MiCA crypto authorization race as Europe’s deadline looms
summary: Germany leads MiCA crypto authorization race as Europe’s deadline looms
briefing: MiCA crypto licensing data shows 244 approved companies across EU and EEA jurisdictions, led by Germany with France and the Netherlands also among top hubs ahead of July 1 cliff.

title: BTC price RSI prints key 2026 signal: Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
summary: BTC price RSI prints key 2026 signal: Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
briefing: Bitcoin prepped an "interesting" RSI divergence into the Q2 and June candle close as analysis compared $60,000 to the $30,000 mark in the 2022 bear market.

title: Strategy Could Sell Up to $1.25B of Bitcoin Under 'Digital Credit Capital Framework'
summary: Strategy Could Sell Up to $1.25B of Bitcoin Under 'Digital Credit Capital Framework'
briefing: The Bitcoin treasury firm has approved a framework for "active capital management," its Chair Michael Saylor said in a statement.

title: Morning Minute: Bitcoin Headed for Rare Back-to-Back Quarterly Loss
summary: Morning Minute: Bitcoin Headed for Rare Back-to-Back Quarterly Loss
briefing: Another $1.79B in ETF outflows last week certainly isn't helping—but if history repeats, there is reason for hope.

title: Ukraine Takes $8.3M in Seized Crypto Under State Management in a First
summary: Ukraine Takes $8.3M in Seized Crypto Under State Management in a First
briefing: Over 8.3 million USDT seized from an alleged hacking ring has been moved to a wallet held by Ukraine's asset-recovery agency, ARMA.

title: The Future Cyberpunk Imagined Is Here: How Much Did It Get Right?
summary: The Future Cyberpunk Imagined Is Here: How Much Did It Get Right?
briefing: From AI to brain implants, cyberpunk got more right than many people realize. Its biggest prediction, however, wasn't the technology.

title: The Stablecoin Founder Map Doesn't Match the Stablecoin Volume Map
summary: The Stablecoin Founder Map Doesn't Match the Stablecoin Volume Map
briefing: Emerging markets drive most real-world stablecoin usage, yet founder concentration and venture funding remain U.S.- and Europe-centric.

title: Billionaire Jeremy Grantham Dismisses Bitcoin, Says Crypto Will Fade 'With a Whimper'
summary: Billionaire Jeremy Grantham Dismisses Bitcoin, Says Crypto Will Fade 'With a Whimper'
briefing: The seasoned investor has little faith in Bitcoin's staying power, expecting crypto to quietly fade away over time.

title: Wall Street's Next Tokenization Test: BlackRock-Backed Securitize's Market Debut
summary: Wall Street's Next Tokenization Test: BlackRock-Backed Securitize's Market Debut
briefing: Securitize expects to begin trading next week under the ticker symbol “SECZ,” following the completion of a merger with a blank-check firm.

title: Linux Foundation, Tech Giants Launch Akrites to Defend Open Source Against AI-Powered Attacks
summary: Linux Foundation, Tech Giants Launch Akrites to Defend Open Source Against AI-Powered Attacks
briefing: A coalition of 19 organizations—including every major AI lab and Wall Street banks—just built the security team that open-source maintainers never had.
